steps.title: Use the visible graph points
steps.content: From the graph, use the marked lattice points $(-5,-1)$ and $(2,4)$. These are enough to determine the line without inventing any extra points.

steps.title: Find the line equation
steps.content:
The slope is:
$$m=\frac{4-(-1)}{2-(-5)}=\frac{5}{7}$$
Use point-slope form with $(2,4)$:
$$y-4=\frac{5}{7}(x-2)$$
Multiply by $7$: $$7y-28=5x-10$$
Rearrange: $$5x-7y=-18$$
Multiplying by $7$ gives $$35x-49y=-126$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content:
Verify both visible points in choice B:
$35(2)-49(4)=70-196=-126$
$35(-5)-49(-1)=-175+49=-126$
Both points satisfy the equation, so B is correct.
